summary: Iteration 2 P1/P2 features tested end-to-end. All 25 backend tests pass (RBAC, forgot/reset, OTP + step-up, schedules incl. background loop dispatch verified in 100s, file uploads with attachment, prompt-injection sanitizer). Frontend flows verified: /forgot confirmation, /reset token pre-fill, Approvals→OTP dialog→approve, Settings schedules table (6 rows) with toggle, Finance/Compliance file uploaders wired.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 950 lines — consider splitting into routers/modules (auth, agents, files, schedules) for maintainability.

seed() has duplicate schedules insertion path (backfill block + first-time block) — both call insert_many for schedules; if users are being seeded fresh, schedules are inserted twice (backfill sees count==0 → inserts, then first-time block inserts again). Consider removing the duplicate insert inside the fresh-seed block.
sanitize_input regex pattern for 'you are ... ai' is very broad and could over-filter legitimate goals mentioning AI capabilities.
Employee role can call GET /approvals (returns all approvals without filter). Consider restricting to own or hiding from employee UI.
request_otp returns demo_hint whenever RESEND_API_KEY empty — acceptable for demo but should be gated behind a DEBUG flag before production.
seed_data_creation: Test suite creates temporary TEST_reset_<uuid>@acos.io users via /auth/register; not cleaned up (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py covers all P1/P2 flows including the slow scheduler_loop test (~100s). RESEND_API_KEY intentionally empty, so demo_hint carries the OTP. Manager approve path modifies pending approvals — running the suite consumes 2 of 3 seeded pending approvals. Re-seed DB (drop test_database.approvals) between runs if you need pending items.
